_patch_meta.description: Session B completion patch for registry 23 (compassion). Sets session_b_status to Session B Complete. Analysis document: WA-23-compassion-analysis-20260326.docx (435 paragraphs, 6 parts). 42 working terms analysed. Key families: racham/rachamim (womb-rooted compassion), rechem (womb as etymological ground), chen/chesed/chanan (favour and covenant-loyalty), chamal/chus (enacted pity), nacham (comfort and relenting), splanchnizomai/splanchnon (gut-level compassion in Jesus), elee-/oikte- (mercy/compassion in Paul and Luke). Key finding: compassion is an inner movement before it is an action; the womb metaphor grounds divine compassion as felt, costly, and particularised. Seventh consecutive applicator anomaly corrected by OP-001.

operations.description: RECURRING APPLICATOR ANOMALY — seventh consecutive registry (98, 34, 160, 126, 59, 42, 23). update_registry ops in PREANALYSIS patches continue not executing. OP-001 corrects registry 23 directly. Claude Code must resolve this before any further session work. Pattern now confirmed across the entire current session.

Session B analysis complete for registry 23 (compassion). Document: WA-23-compassion-analysis-20260326.docx. Working set: 42 terms (21 extracted, 21 extracted_thin | Hebrew 29, Greek 13). 2 volume flags: PH2-023-001 (G1656 eleos 11%), PH2-023-002 (G3628 oiktirmos 14%). 1 data error: PH2-023-003 (H0854 et — seventh consecutive extraction anomaly). Cross-registry notes: chesed spans compassion (23), faith (59), delight (42); racham/shalom linkage in Isa 54:10 for Session D.
